sections.heading: Overview
body: Monophonic bass synth based on Robin Schmidt's Open303 engine,
with midilab's Devilfish-inspired extensions and GuitarML's
No sequencer — drive it with any MIDI source. Pairs well with
TB-3PO in a shadow slot for generative acid basslines.

sections.heading: Knobs (8 front panel)
body: 7  Drive Level  — overdrive pre-gain (also input for

sections.heading: MIDI
body: Notes: standard note-on / note-off drive pitch and gate.
Accent: velocity ≥ 100 triggers Open303's accent envelope.
Slide:  overlapping notes (next note-on before previous
note-off) triggers legato slide — matches TB-3PO's
CC map (honors GM2/MPE conventions where applicable):

sections.heading: Devilfish Mods
body: Off: stock 303 behavior. Mod knobs are inert.
On:  Devilfish-inspired extensions from the real TB-303 mod
kit, implemented in the Open303 engine.
Normal Decay   30 ms – 3 s (stock: fixed ~1.2 s)
Accent Decay   30 ms – 3 s (stock: fixed 200 ms)
Feedback HPF   cuts bass from filter self-feedback
Soft Attack    0.3 ms – 3 s attack for non-accented notes
Shaper Drive   tanh waveshaper before the filter

sections.heading: Overdrive
body: Neural-network amp/pedal modelling via RTNeural. 37 classic
amp/pedal captures (TS9, RAT, Big Muff, Princeton, Mesa Mini
Rec, Dumble Kit, etc.) — the jc303 default set.
On:   signal runs through the chosen model. Use Drive Level
to push harder; use Dry/Wet to blend with clean.

sections.heading: Credits
body: Neural overdrive: GuitarML BYOD (MIT) via RTNeural (BSD-3)
Tone models: GuitarML Proteus Tone Packs
